text: The capital of France is Paris. Paris sits on the river Seine and is famous for the Eiffel Tower.
The capital of Germany is Berlin. Berlin is known for the Brandenburg Gate and a rich modern history.
The capital of Japan is Tokyo. Tokyo is the largest metropolitan area in the world by population.
The capital of Italy is Rome. Rome was the centre of the ancient Roman Empire and is full of historic ruins.
The capital of Spain is Madrid. Madrid is home to the Royal Palace and the Prado Museum.
The capital of the United Kingdom is London. London is built along the river Thames and home to the British Museum.
The capital of Australia is Canberra. Canberra was purpose-built as the federal capital in the early twentieth century.
The capital of Canada is Ottawa. Ottawa lies on the Ottawa River across from Gatineau in Quebec.
The capital of Brazil is Brasilia. Brasilia was inaugurated in 1960 as a modernist planned city.
The capital of Egypt is Cairo. Cairo is the largest city in the Arab world and home to the Egyptian Museum.
The Earth orbits the Sun once every 365.25 days, which is why we add a leap day every four years.
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure and freezes at 0 degrees Celsius.
Mount Everest is the tallest mountain on Earth above sea level, rising 8,848 metres in the Himalayas.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest of Earth's five oceans, covering more than 30 percent of the planet's surface.
Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide into glucose and oxygen.
